document_identity.franchisor_address: 7930 Jones Branch Drive, Suite 1100, McLean, Virginia 22102

brand_info.prototype_description: 4-floor, double-loaded central hallway, 55K-63K sqft, 100 rooms, 1 lobby, 2 elevators, outdoor parking ~100 vehicles. No kitchens, restaurants, pools, health facilities, or dedicated meeting rooms.

brand_leader.name: Alissa Klees
brand_leader.title: VP and Brand Leader – Spark by Hilton

pending_actions: AAAA Property Partners v. Hilton (breach of contract, HGI termination)
In re Extended Stay Hotel Antitrust Litigation (class action, IDeaS price-fixing)
Hanson Dai v. SAS Institute (class action, IDeaS price-fixing)
Ryan Segal v. Amadeus (class action, Amadeus data exchange)
Jeanette Portillo v. CoStar/STR (class action, STR data exchange)
notable_settlements: Hawaii resort fee class action: $178K + vouchers (Aug 2015)

initial_fees.hsm_procurement_fee: Up to 10% of cost of goods

ongoing_fees.program_fee_rate: 3.0% of GRR (3.5% if not in compliance)

noncompete.during_term: Cannot own/be franchisor of competing brand

dispute_resolution.forum: ED Virginia or Fairfax County VA or New York NY

item19.no_fpr_statement: We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised outlets.

unresolveds.description: Note 9 subsequent events evaluated through 'March 18, 2024' — likely typo for 2025
California shows +1 'ceased operations – other reasons' in 2024 for a brand only 2 years old — unusual
Exhibit N (Adyen Agreement) listed in TOC/receipts but not clearly demarcated as separate exhibit in text layer

California.override_summary: Liquidated damages may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code 1671
CA Business and Professions Code 20000-20043 provides termination/non-renewal rights
NY governing law provision may not be enforceable in California
Virginia venue requirement may not be enforceable in California
CA Corp Code 31512 voids waiver of rights under Franchise Investment Law

Hawaii.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from liability under Hawaii franchising laws

Illinois.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs IL licensees; venue/choice of law/jury waiver provisions do not waive IL rights
Settlement agreements and arbitration still permitted

Maryland.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA termination/renewal provisions
May sue in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Law; 3-year statute of limitations
General release does not release liability under MD Franchise Law

Michigan.override_summary: Good cause required for termination; 30 days to cure
Must fairly compensate on non-renewal (FMV repurchase) if term <5 years
Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan prohibited
Cannot refuse transfer except for good cause

Minnesota.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 to cure), 180 days notice of non-renewal
Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota or waive jury trial
General release does not relieve liability under MN Franchise Investment Law

New_York.override_summary: NY Franchises Law requires NY law govern causes of action under NY Franchise Law
General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law

North_Dakota.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law; FA governed by ND law
Release does not relieve liability under ND Franchise Disclosure Act

Rhode_Island.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal does not apply to claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
Forum/venue restrictions outside RI and non-RI choice of law void

Virginia.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act

Washington.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA termination/renewal provisions
Release excludes rights under WA Act except in negotiated settlements
Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/yr and contractors <$250K/yr (adjusted for inflation)
Arbitration/mediation site must be in WA or mutually agreed; litigation may be brought in WA
